datasets.name: dataset_scored
queryLines: SELECT sales_id, stuck_region, state, city, car_name, model, fuel, days_on_market, aging_tier, original_selling_price, at_risk_revenue, risk_score, demand_region, demand_median_days, recommended_action, priority_rank FROM primeins.gold.v_alert_scored

datasets.name: dataset_kpi
queryLines: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_unsold_cars, COUNT(CASE WHEN aging_tier = 'CRITICAL' THEN 1 END) AS critical_cars, COUNT(CASE WHEN aging_tier = 'HIGH' THEN 1 END) AS high_cars, COUNT(CASE WHEN aging_tier = 'WATCH' THEN 1 END) AS watch_cars, ROUND(SUM(at_risk_revenue), 0) AS total_at_risk_revenue FROM primeins.gold.v_aging_inventory

datasets.name: dataset_redistribution
queryLines: WITH ranked AS (SELECT stuck_region, aging_tier, sales_id, at_risk_revenue, demand_region, demand_median_days, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY stuck_region, aging_tier ORDER BY demand_median_days ASC) AS rn FROM primeins.gold.v_alert_scored WHERE aging_tier IN ('CRITICAL', 'HIGH') AND demand_region IS NOT NULL) SELECT stuck_region, aging_tier, COUNT(DISTINCT sales_id) AS unsold_cars, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT at_risk_revenue), 0) AS at_risk_revenue, COUNT(DISTINCT sales_id) AS redistributable_cars, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT at_risk_revenue), 0) AS recoverable_revenue, MAX(CASE WHEN rn = 1 THEN CONCAT(demand_region, ' (~', CAST(demand_median_days AS INT), ' days)') END) AS best_destination FROM ranked GROUP BY stuck_region, aging_tier ORDER BY aging_tier, at_risk_revenue DESC

datasets.name: dataset_model_breakdown
queryLines: SELECT model, fuel, COUNT(*) AS unsold_cars, ROUND(SUM(at_risk_revenue), 0) AS total_at_risk_revenue, ROUND(AVG(days_on_market), 1) AS avg_days_on_market FROM primeins.gold.v_aging_inventory WHERE aging_tier IN ('CRITICAL', 'HIGH') GROUP BY model, fuel ORDER BY total_at_risk_revenue DESC LIMIT 15

datasets.name: dataset_aging_distribution
queryLines: SELECT aging_tier, COUNT(*) AS car_count, ROUND(SUM(at_risk_revenue), 0) AS revenue_at_risk FROM primeins.gold.v_aging_inventory GROUP BY aging_tier ORDER BY CASE aging_tier WHEN 'CRITICAL' THEN 1 WHEN 'HIGH' THEN 2 WHEN 'WATCH' THEN 3 ELSE 4 END

datasets.name: dataset_recoverable_kpi
queryLines: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT sales_id) AS redistributable_cars, ROUND(SUM(DISTINCT at_risk_revenue), 0) AS recoverable_revenue FROM primeins.gold.v_alert_scored WHERE demand_region IS NOT NULL AND aging_tier IN ('CRITICAL', 'HIGH')

lines:
# PrimeIns — Unsold Inventory Intelligence
**Source:** `primeins.gold`   |   **Refreshed:** Daily at 08:00   |   **Owner:** PrimeIns Data Team

This dashboard tracks unsold car inventory across all regions. It identifies cars aging past safe thresholds, quantifies revenue at risk from forced discounts, and surfaces redistribution opportunities to recover that revenue before the discount window closes.

**Aging tiers:**   **CRITICAL** = 90+ days — act immediately  |  **HIGH** = 60–90 days — plan this week  |  **WATCH** = 30–60 days — monitor
### Total Unsold Cars
All cars currently unsold across every region and tier
### Critical — 90+ Days
Past the discount window. Redistribute or markdown immediately
### Revenue at Risk (₹)
Estimated 15% markdown loss if all unsold cars are force-discounted
### High Risk — 60–90 Days
Plan redistribution this week before crossing the 90-day threshold
### Watch — 30–60 Days
Monitor weekly. Not urgent yet but trending toward risk
### Recoverable Revenue (₹)
Revenue saveable by redistributing cars before forced discounting

---
## Inventory Breakdown by Region and Tier
The charts below show how unsold cars and revenue risk are distributed across regions. Use these to identify which regions need the most urgent attention.

# Redistribution Opportunities
An unsold car in one region is not necessarily unsellable — it may simply be in the wrong place. This page identifies cars stuck in one region with **proven demand** in another, based on how fast the same model and fuel type sold there in the last 90 days.

**Best Destination** = the region where that model sold fastest (lowest median days to sell). Moving cars there before the 90-day window closes avoids forced discounting and recovers revenue that would otherwise be written off.

> Focus on **CRITICAL** rows first — these are past the 90-day threshold.
### Recoverable Revenue (₹)
Revenue saveable by moving cars to a higher-demand region before forced markdown
### Cars That Can Be Moved
Unsold cars with at least one matching demand region identified — prioritise these first
### Regions Needing Action
Distinct regions with CRITICAL or HIGH cars that have a viable redistribution destination

---
## Action Table — Where to Move Each Region's Stock
Each row is one region. **Best Destination** shows the fastest-selling region for that region's model mix. Sort by **Revenue at Risk** descending to prioritise the highest-value moves first.

# Car-Level Detail
Every unsold car ranked by composite risk score. Use this page to drill into specific cars, identify which models are most at risk, and understand the fuel-type distribution of stuck inventory.

**Risk Score** = 50% days aging + 30% selling price + 20% redistribution opportunity bonus. Sort by **Rank** ascending to see the most urgent cars first.

**Move To** = best destination region for that car. If blank, no demand signal was found — a pricing review is recommended instead.
### Top Models by Revenue at Risk
Which car models are contributing most to the leakage problem. Brief the sourcing team on which models to avoid overstocking in slow regions
### Avg Days on Market by Fuel Type
Whether certain fuel types are systematically slower to sell — may indicate a regional demand or pricing mismatch

---
## Full Car-Level Risk Register
Every unsold car with its risk score, recommended action, and destination region. **Note:** one car may appear multiple times if it matches demand in more than one region — each row is a separate redistribution option. Use the **Rank** column to identify the single best action per car.
